title: Investigate the failures in /tmp/phase0-1c8023 for test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs and test-e2e-tier-skip.mjs. Determine whether each failure is caused by a code regression, stale test expectations, or environment assumptions. Inspect delegate.js, fugue-execute.mjs, and relevant tests. Do not edit file...
summary_text: Findings: 1. `test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs` Classification: **Environment assumption** (not a delegate regression) - The test harness creates `codex` as a Node shebang script at [test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs:20](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs:20)-[27](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs:27), then overrides `HOME` and `PATH` at [44](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs:44)-[46](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs:46). - In this environment, `/usr/bin/env node` from that shim resolves to a `node-wrapper` that fails before running the shim, so `codex-args.json` is never written; read at [52](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-delegate-mcp-config.mjs:52) throws `ENOENT`. - Delegate MCP behavior itself is correct in code: default inherit at [delegate.js:566](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/delegate.js:566)-[570](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/delegate.js:570). Recommended fix: - Make fake `codex` a POSIX shell script (no Node shebang dependency), or add a local `bin/node -> process.execPath` shim in the test harness before creating fake `codex`. - Add a clearer assertion before JSON parse (assert args file exists; include child stderr in failure). 2. `test-e2e-tier-skip.mjs` Classification: **Environment assumption** (state-path/writeability dependency), not stale expectations - Test spawns child with inherited env only at [test-e2e-tier-skip.mjs:9](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-e2e-tier-skip.mjs:9)-[10](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-e2e-tier-skip.mjs:10). - `--dry-run` still persists run state at [fugue-execute.mjs:666](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:666)-[672](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:672). - Run-state path is hardcoded to `os.homedir()/.claude/state` at [run-state.js:9](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/run-state.js:9)-[15](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/run-state.js:15), with writes at [43](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/run-state.js:43)-[47](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/run-state.js:47). In restricted environments this causes `EPERM` and test failure. - The expected `status=done` output format is still produced when environment is writable/stable. Recommended fix: - In test, run child with isolated writable home (`HOME=<tmpdir>`) and stable Node binary on `PATH` (or explicit absolute `process.execPath` launcher). - Hardening option in code: support `FUGUE_STATE_DIR`/`FUGUE_HOME` override in [run-state.js](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/run-state.js), so tests don’t depend on global `~/.claude/state`.
